regions.name: Bangkok
regions.description: Thailand's vibrant capital — ornate temples, rooftop bars, street food markets, and the Grand Palace
regions.bestFor: Temple tours, street food, nightlife, shopping

regions.name: Chiang Mai
regions.description: Cultural capital of northern Thailand — ancient walled city, elephant sanctuaries, and mountain trekking
regions.bestFor: Culture, nature, cooking classes, ethical wildlife experiences

regions.name: Phuket & Krabi
regions.description: Southern Thailand's island paradise — white sand beaches, limestone karsts, turquoise water, and island hopping
regions.bestFor: Beaches, snorkelling, island hopping, water sports

attractions.name: Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew
attractions.description: Bangkok's most sacred site — the former royal residence with the revered Emerald Buddha temple

attractions.name: Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha)
attractions.description: Home to the 46-metre gold-plated Reclining Buddha and the birthplace of traditional Thai massage

attractions.name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
attractions.description: One of the world's largest weekend markets — 15,000 stalls covering clothes, art, food, and antiques

attractions.name: Chao Phraya River & Temples Tour
attractions.description: Long-tail boat ride visiting Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) and river-side temples from the water

attractions.name: Doi Suthep Temple
attractions.description: Sacred golden temple perched 1,073m above Chiang Mai — 309 naga-flanked steps lead to panoramic views

attractions.name: Elephant Nature Park
attractions.description: Ethical elephant sanctuary — observe, feed, and bathe rescued elephants without riding

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Old City & Temples
attractions.description: Ancient moat-encircled city with 300 temples — Wat Chedi Luang, Wat Phra Singh, and serene back alleys

attractions.name: Phi Phi Islands Day Trip
attractions.description: Speedboat to the stunning Phi Phi archipelago — snorkel at Maya Bay, swim in crystal waters

attractions.name: Railay Beach
attractions.description: Stunning peninsula accessible only by boat — dramatic limestone cliffs, crystal waters, rock climbing

attractions.name: Phang Nga Bay (James Bond Island)
attractions.description: Dramatic karst limestone islands rising from emerald waters — sea kayaking through caves

january.weather: Dry and cool in north (20-28°C), warm in south (27-32°C)
packingTips: Light clothes with a layer for northern evenings
pros: Best weather of the year — dry and comfortable everywhere

notableEvents: Songkran Water Festival (Thai New Year, April 13-15)

july.weather: Rainy season — monsoon rains, 25-33°C, daily afternoon downpours
cons: Heavy rains can disrupt beach plans

october.weather: Transitioning — rain ending in north, still rainy in south
pros: Loy Krathong lantern festival is magical
Transitioning to dry season in Bangkok/north
cons: South can still have heavy rain

culturalTips: Dress modestly at all temples — cover shoulders and knees, remove shoes before entering
Never touch or point your feet at a Buddha image or a person — feet are considered the lowest part of the body
The royal family is deeply revered — avoid any negative comments about the monarchy (lèse-majesté laws)
Bargaining is expected at markets but always with a smile — aggressive haggling is rude
Thai people greet with a 'wai' (pressing palms together and bowing slightly) — reciprocate respectfully
Street food is generally safe and delicious — look for stalls with high turnover and local customers
Tipping is appreciated — 20-50 baht for most services, 10% at sit-down restaurants
Elephant riding is widely considered cruel — choose observation-only ethical sanctuaries

budget.area: Khao San Road (Bangkok) or Nimman (Chiang Mai)
budget.description: Backpacker hub with hostels and guesthouses at $15-50/night

luxury.description: 5-star resorts with river views, infinity pools, spa, $180-400/night
